steps.title: Identify the key difference
steps.content: The notes present one fundamental contrast: surface-dwelling tetras “are able to see” while cave-dwelling tetras “have lost the ability to see.” This vision difference is the clearest and most directly stated distinction between the two populations.

steps.title: Match the comparison structure to the choices
steps.content: D uses the contrast word whereas to directly emphasize the difference by juxtaposing opposite abilities: surface-dwelling tetras can see, cave-dwelling tetras cannot. The other three don't create this comparative emphasis — A vaguely states they're “quite different” without specifying how, B focuses on only the cave-dwelling population without contrast, and C merely identifies that the populations exist. That matches choice D — D is correct.
